diagrams.description: This timeline traces the intellectual and institutional history of Criminology. It begins with the classical school (Beccaria's 'On Crimes and Punishments', 1764), moves to the positivist school with Lombroso's 'The Criminal Man' (1876). The early 20th century saw the Chicago School and social disorganization theory (1920s‑30s). Edwin Sutherland introduced differential association (1939) and coined 'white‑collar crime'. The mid‑20th century produced strain theory (Merton, 1938), social control theory (Hirschi, 1969), and labeling theory (Becker, 1963). The late 20th century saw critical criminology, left realism, and feminist criminology. Recent decades emphasize developmental/life‑course criminology, environmental criminology, and evidence‑based policy.
